data.title: Iron ore and gold: two defensive plays over the next 12-18 months amid tariffs risk
data.description:
Investors should look at iron ore (SCO:COM) and gold (GLD), (XAUUSD:CUR) as defensive buys over the next year to 18 months as the upcoming Trump administration plans to increase tariffs that could affect trade.

Tom Price, managing director of resources at Panmure Liberum, said in a CNBC interview that former President Trump has already given a playbook for commodities in 2018-2019.

He said that there were only two commodities that did really well, gold (GLD), (XAUUSD:CUR), and iron ore (SCO:COM), outperformed gold during that time.

“China freaked out and said, ‘oh, wow, tariffs, okay, we better beef up investment in our own economy,’” he said. “So, they went down the steel intensive route. So, the iron trade boomed, and so did the price.”

In 2018, iron ore’s global production saw a slight increase, driven by increased demand from China. The U.S. saw a 3% rise in domestic iron ore production that year.

According to the United States Geological Survey, global iron ore production was 2.46B metric tons of usable ore, led by Australia with about 900 metric tons, Brazil with 460 metric tons, China with 335 metric tons, India with 205 metric tons and Russia with 96.1 metric tons – all together accounting for 81% of global production.

In 2019, China’s iron ore imports rose 0.47% hovering just below their all-time annual peak.

Data from the General Administration of Customs showed that China brought in 1.069B tons of iron ore in 2019, up from 1.064B tons in 2018.

Iron ore stocks:

  • Rio Tinto Group (RIO)
  • Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF)
  • Gerdau S.A. (GGB)
  • Black Iron (OTCPK:BKIRF)
  • Steel Dynamics (STLD)
  • Mitsui & Co (OTCPK:MITSY)
  • U.S. Steel (X)

  • Fortescue (OTCQX:FSUMF)
  • BHP Group (BHP)

data.title: Crypto titan Do Kwon pleads not guilty to criminal fraud charges - report
data.description:
Do Kwon, the South Korean crypto mogul bring behind the multibillion-dollar Terraform (UST-USD) collapse in 2022, pleaded not guilty to criminal fraud charges after his extradition to the U.S. on Dec. 31, according to a media report dated Friday.

Federal prosecutors have claimed that, in 2021, Kwon provided misleading information to investors regarding the algorithm intended to keep the value oif the stablecoin terraUSD (UST-USD) pegged at $1, Reuters reported, citing Thursday's indictment.

Kwon has been ordered by Magistrate Judge Robert W. Lehrburger for the Southern District of New York to be detained before returning to court on Jan. 8, the article said.

In March 2023, Kwon was arrested by police in Montenegro on passport forgery charges.

data.title: General Motors' 2024 vehicle sales increase by 4% to 2.7M
data.description:
• General Motors (NYSE:GM) sold 4% more vehicles in 2024, reaching a 5-year record of 2.7M, with sales for all the company’s brands improving from a year ago.
  • For Q4, GM sales were up 21% as sales of EVs climbed 50% led by double-digit gains in the electric Equinox and Traverse.
  • The average transaction price increased to $53K while incentive spending per vehicle continued to decline.
  • In 2024, Chevy sales were up 1.5%, GMC sales increased 9%, Buick sales showed a 10% improvement, while Cadillac sales increased 9% on strength in its Escalade SUV.

data.title: Block stock climbs after upgrade to Outperform at Raymond James
data.description:
Block (NYSE:SQ) shares advanced 5.3% in Friday midday trading after Raymond James analyst John Davis raised his rating recommendation on the stock to Outperform from Market Perform on the basis of a potential acceleration in Seller gross payment volume growth.

Davis expects Seller GPV growth to rebound into the double-digits in 2025, from 8% in 2024, due to the combination of easing comparisons, increased sales momentum, international expansion and product innovation, he wrote in a note to clients.

As such, he added, "we acknowledge Cash App is a bit of a wild card given net new actives have been flat over the last two quarters, although we believe the emphasis on driving engagement within the existing customer base should insulate gross profit growth if lackluster user growth persists."

RayJay's Outperform rate aligns with the SA Quant system rating, the average SA analyst rating and the average Wall Street analyst rating, each at Buy.

data.title: Salesforce is Raymond James' top software stock for 2025 as firm tweaks ratings
data.description:
The enterprise software space is expected to have a momentous 2025, investment firm Raymond James said in a recent research note. And while Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) is the firm's top pick in the space, it made a number of ratings changes to other enterprise companies at the start of the new year.

The firm downgraded Intapp (NASDAQ:INTA) and Vertex (NASDAQ:VERX) to Market Perform from Outperform and also cut its rating on RingCentral (NYSE:RNG) to Outperform from Strong Buy, along with a $45 price target. Conversely, it upgraded Workiva (WK) to Outperform from Market Perform to Outperform with a $135 price target.

Analysts at the firm noted that artificial intelligence has dominated the tech narrative for the past 18 to 24 months, but the impact on enterprise software has resulted in more questions than answers, as investors wondered what markets could be disrupted or how seat-based models would work.

But as the initial generative AI use cases proved to be more "Copilot oriented," the narrative looks to have changed, at least from a monetization angle, the analysts said.

"We think that narrative has changed in 2024 with front-end software vendors such as Salesforce, HubSpot, ZoomInfo, Freshworks and Intapp all in the market with monetizable GenAI products (including others like OneStream)," the analysts wrote in a note to clients.

"We also believe that the domain specific function provided by agents will allow for much better application functionality than what’s offered by Co-Pilots, with Agents augmenting the human workforce across multiple areas."

As such, the firm's top pick in the large cap enterprise software space is Salesforce, with the analysts saying the company is "on pace" to deliver roughly 10% subscription growth on a $30B revenue base, notable given that companies are increasingly turning to Salesforce for multi-cloud transformation projects.

"Given market leadership, potential for continued double-digit FCF growth, and perhaps the most differentiated AI narrative in our coverage, all at a group-average multiple, we would bet on CRM as a compounder in 2025," Raymond James analysts wrote in a note to clients.

The firm also likes Waystar Holding (WAY) and Weave Communications (WEAV) in the mid-cap and small-cap spaces, respectively.

data.title: Fed's Barkin sees still-restrictive rates lowering inflation in 2025
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond President Tom Barkin judged that interest rates remain sufficiently restrictive to further lower inflation in 2025, he said on Friday.

“Inflation is not yet back to target, so we still have more work to do, but we don’t think we need to be nearly as restrictive as we once were to finish that job,” Barkin, who voted in favor of a 25-basis-point Fed rate cut at the December Federal Open Market Committee meeting, said in remarks prepared for delivery to the Maryland Bankers Association in Linthicum Heights.

Overall, Barkin laid out a positive 2025 outlook for the U.S. economy. "A strong but choosier consumer, coupled with a better-valued, more productive workforce has landed the economy in a good place," he said.

The Richmond Fed chief said he sees more positive surprises than negative ones in economic growth and believes the current labor market balance is more likely to shift toward increased hiring rather than layoffs.

And higher inflation is an even greater risk, Barkin said, warning "wage and product costs could see pressure. “If they do, given recent experience with inflation, price-setters might have more courage to pass costs along.”

Last month, Fed Chair Jerome Powell said the policy rate, of which the target range of which stands at 4.25%-4.50%, was "now significantly less restrictive" following 100 basis points worth of monetary easing since September.

data.title: No S&P 500 stocks are overbought, but these are the 10 most oversold names
data.description:
Out of the 500 companies listed in the S&P 500 index (SP500), none are currently deemed overbought according to the relative strength index (RSI) technical indicator. However, a total of 51 companies within the index are classified as overbought when evaluated using the RSI.

For reference, an RSI indicator reading above 70 suggests a stock is overbought or potentially overvalued, while an RSI below 30 indicates it is oversold or potentially undervalued.

Listed below are the S&P 500’s ten most oversold stocks per the RSI indicator.

No. 10: HCA Healthcare (HCA), RSI reading of 23.64.

No. 9: Nucor Corp (NUE), RSI reading of 23.46.

No. 8: Builders Firstsource (BLDR), RSI reading of 23.37.

No. 7: Lennar Corp (LEN), RSI reading of 22.92.

No. 6: Illinois Tool Works (ITW), RSI reading of 22.88.

No. 5: W.W. Grainger Inc. (GWW), RSI reading of 22.59.

No. 4: Fastenal (FAST), RSI reading of 22.34.

No. 3: Steel Dynamics (STLD), RSI reading of 21.97.

No. 2: Nordson Corp (NDSN), RSI reading of 21.76.

No. 1: Crown Castle (Inc. (CCI), RSI reading of 19.76.

Additionally, see how Seeking Alpha quant grades measured each of the above ten names: HCA 3.02, NUE 1.75, BLDR 2.69, LEN 2.80, ITW 3.30, GWW 3.27, FAST 2.98, STLD 2.63, NDSN 1.70, and CCI 1.30.

Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics.

data.title: Best and worst performing S&P 500 stocks in a rosy year for index
data.description:
The S&P 500 had an outstanding 2024, gaining over 20% over the year and for the second consecutive year. The last time it did so was 27 years ago.

The index was driven by Fed rate cuts, the U.S. Presidential election and an investing frenzy in AI. Easing inflation indicators and other favorable macroeconomic reports – including jobs and consumption figures – further buoyed the market.

Consequently, the tech sector was a big winner in the S&P 500 index, with Nvidia (NVDA) being arguably the biggest of them with a gain of around +178%. Others in the Magnificent 7 also had a stellar 2024, with Microsoft (MSFT) +13%, Apple (AAPL) +31%, Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL) +37%, Amazon (AMZN) +46%, and Meta Platforms (META) and Tesla (TSLA) +67%.

Communication services (XLC) made the largest gain among S&P 500 sectors, rising nearly 34% over 2024. The financial sector had a solid year too, increasing almost 28%. Consumer discretionary and information technology sectors were both up 26% and 24%, respectively.

Looking ahead to 2025, analysts appear most positive about the communication, energy and information technology sectors.

Here were the 10 best-performing stocks from the index in 2024:

  • Palantir Technologies (PLTR): 2024 growth +356.15%
  • Vistra (VST): 2024 growth +262.15%
  • Nvidia (NVDA): 2024 growth +178.8%
  • GE Vernova (GEV): 2024 growth +150.61%
  • United Airlines (UAL): 2024 growth +138.46%
  • Axon Enterprise (AXON): 2024 growth +136.19%
  • Broadcom (AVGO): 2024 growth +133.60%
  • Texas Pacific Land (TPL): 2024 growth +106.77%
  • Targa Resources (TRGP): 2024 growth +106.55%
  • Howmet Aerospace (HWM): 2024 growth +103.55%

Here are the 10 worst-performing S&P 500 stocks in 2024:

  • Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA): -64.99%
  • Moderna (MRNA): -63%
  • Intel (INTC): -58%
  • Celanese (CE): -55.27%
  • The Estée Lauder Companies (EL): -48.45%
  • Enphase Energy (ENPH): -47.67%
  • Dollar Tree (DLTR): -47.43%
  • Humana (HUM): -46.03%
  • Dollar General (DG): -46.01%
  • CVS Health (CVS): -44.48%

More on the biggest S&P 500 movers of 2024

  • Moderna: From Breakthrough COVID-19 Vaccines To Personalized Cancer Therapies
  • Palantir: Applaud The Fundamentals, But Short Valuation

data.title: Viking Therapeutics among potential buyout candidates: Oppenheimer
data.description:
Ahead of the 2025 J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference scheduled to take place in a few days, Oppenheimer named five potential buyout candidates in biotech on Friday, naming obesity drug developer Viking Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VKTX) in the list.

In addition to VKTX, Biegler included Blueprint Medicines (NASDAQ:BPMC), Janux Therapeutics (NASDAQ:JANX), Arcellx (NASDAQ:ACLX), and Nuvalent (NUVL) in the group.

"The second week of January tends to be a bellwether for biotech M&A and a broader gauge of sector sentiment," analyst Matthew Biegler wrote, as the JPM conference is expected to get underway in San Francisco, California, on January 13-16.

"We need more M&A, and not the garden variety we've gotten since COVID," he added, noting that the biotech sentiment has fallen short of expectations this year.

The analyst said all five stocks are "large enough to make headlines," and an M&A deal or two for them could reverse the current biotech sentiment.

"We think a takeout or two (below) could catalyze a reversal of the XBI's fortunes," Biegler wrote, referring to the industry benchmark, SPDR S&P Biotech ETF (XBI).

With an Outperform recommendation on Viking (NASDAQ:VKTX), the analyst named the company one of the few investable stocks in obesity outside Eli Lilly (LLY), Novo Nordisk (NVO), and Amgen (AMGN).

The San Diego, California-based biotech, which is advancing a promising obesity pill called VK2735, is a leader in the development of oral obesity drugs, Biegler argued, adding that "a takeout would show us that big pharma is willing to pay for a leadership position."

According to Oppenheimer, a large pharma company with operations in immunology and inflammation (I&I) could pursue Blueprint Medicines (NASDAQ:BPMC), which markets Ayvakit, the only FDA-approved treatment for a rare disorder called systemic mastocytosis.

"If SM market is worth $2B, we're not sure why a large pharma hasn't pounced," the analyst wrote with an outperform recommendation on BPMC.

While noting that Janux Therapeutics (JNAX) can be expensive in valuation, the analyst highlighted the potential in its bispecific platform, which he said "can readily be adapted to other targets/cancers—and wouldn't it be nice if big pharma agreed?"

Additionally, the analyst said Arcellx (NASDAQ:ACLX) has what many analysts consider a best-in-class CAR-T therapy for multiple myeloma. According to Biegler, the company has witnessed its valuation peaking as Gilead (GILD) shares ownership for its anito-cel MM therapy. "While a takeout by Gilead would be nice, we think a takeout by someone else would be even better."

On Nuvalent (NUVL), the analyst said the company owns a best-in-class ALK inhibitor, according to Wall Street analysts. "A takeout at current valuations would essentially imply patients staying on drug for years at a time."

data.title: Zymeworks up after sizable purchase by large shareholder
data.description:
• Zymeworks (NASDAQ:ZYME) is up ~3% in Friday trading following the disclosure of a large puirchase of shares by a more than 10% owner.
  • An SEC filing posted Jan. 2 after market close indicates that EcoR1 Capital, which also serves as a director of the company, bought ~363K shares recently in three transactions between $14.47 and $14.78 a share. The purchases total more than $5.3M.
  • EcoR1 now owns ~15.2M shares.
